rows.locks: The M92 demo path produces the Rust-owned import verification report shape with openchrome-local-skeleton-smoke evidence, fidelity counts, provenance, Era R re-derivation tasks, and zero claimed ported units.
Lossy skeleton gaps and behavior-bearing re-derivation work remain Yellow/Red fidelity findings, and validation rejects a tampered report that tries to grade re-derive gaps as clean.

The import verification validator rejects auto-translated claimed_ported_units and oracle-missing records with port permission, so no unit is treated as ported without captured passing oracle evidence.
The import verification state hash is stable for identical canonical source text reports, changes when source data changes, and validation rejects stale hashes after report tampering.

Coverage v81 records the one-way on-ramp, source-project/open-text legal boundary, clean-room Era R re-derivation hand-off, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, no decompiled source copying, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v81 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era O M92 import verification and fidelity reporting. It locks source-project/open-text Godot skeleton import verification, openchrome-local-skeleton-smoke evidence as skeleton-only evidence, no foreign runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash behavior including stale-hash rejection, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
